title: ageratina pentlandiana
intro: Ageratina pentlandiana, also known as Mexican tea or shrubby ironweed, is a shrubby plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Ageratina pentlandiana can be prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its active compounds in a solvent, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine.
